index.alt_definition: To adjust (a price, wage, etc.) in accordance with fluctuations in the cost of living.
index.note: Beyond the common meaning of a list or sign, 'index' can also be a verb related to adjusting values.

table.note: Beyond the furniture definition, it can mean to postpone discussion.

underscore.note: While literally meaning to draw a line under, it's often used figuratively to mean emphasize.

vindicate.alt_definition: To provide justification or support for.
vindicate.note: While it commonly means to clear from blame, it can also mean to justify an action or decision.

check.alt_definition: To restrain or hold back; to stop or slow the progress of.
check.note: Beyond the common meaning of a bank check, it can mean to control or restrain.

qualify.alt_definition: To limit or modify a statement; to make less absolute.
qualify.note: People commonly think of 'meeting requirements,' but it also means to limit a statement.

sanction.alt_definition: To impose a penalty or punishment on; OR to give official approval or permission for.
sanction.note: This word is tricky because it has opposite meanings: to approve AND to penalize.

exploit.alt_definition: To develop or make use of something for commercial or other advantage.
exploit.note: While often negative (selfish use), it can also mean simply to utilize.

denote.alt_definition: To serve as an indication of; to mean.
denote.note: While 'to indicate' is a common meaning, the SAT often uses 'denote' to mean 'to mean' or 'to signify'.

accommodate.alt_definition: To make fit, suitable, or congruous.
accommodate.note: Beyond providing lodging, it can mean to adapt something to fit a specific purpose or requirement.

affront.alt_definition: To insult someone openly or intentionally.
affront.note: While commonly a noun, 'affront' can also be used as a verb meaning to offend.

cite.alt_definition: To summon someone before a court of law.
cite.note: Beyond quoting, 'cite' can also refer to a legal summons, which is a less common usage.

commend.alt_definition: To present as worthy of regard or notice; recommend.
commend.note: While it means to praise, it can also mean to entrust or recommend something to someone's care or attention.

casual.alt_definition: Without definite or serious intention; careless or offhand.
casual.note: Beyond relaxed, it can also imply a lack of seriousness or depth.

discretion.alt_definition: The power or right to decide or act according to one's own judgment.
discretion.note: While it relates to being discreet, it also refers to the freedom to make judgments.

expedite.alt_definition: To issue officially, as in a document or order.
expedite.note: Beyond just 'speeding up,' it can mean to formally issue something.

foment.alt_definition: To apply heat or warm liquids to.
foment.note: While it commonly means to stir up trouble, it also has a literal meaning related to heat.

forestall.alt_definition: To buy up goods in advance in order to increase the price later.
forestall.note: This archaic economic meaning is different from the common 'prevent' definition.

foster.alt_definition: To care for (a child) as a foster parent.
foster.note: Beyond encouraging growth, it refers to providing parental care.

halt.alt_definition: To hesitate or be in doubt.
halt.note: While commonly known as 'to stop', it can also mean to be hesitant.

idle.alt_definition: To run (an engine) without engaging the transmission mechanism.
idle.note: Beyond being inactive, it refers to a specific engine state.

induce.alt_definition: To cause the onset of (labor) artificially, by medication.
induce.note: Beyond persuading, it has a specific medical meaning related to childbirth.

invoke.alt_definition: To put into effect or operation; to implement or apply a law or rule.
invoke.note: Beyond calling upon a deity, 'invoke' can mean to enact or implement something like a law.

modulate.alt_definition: To vary the amplitude, frequency, or phase of (a wave or signal).
modulate.note: While it can mean to adjust, 'modulate' also has a technical meaning related to signals and waves.

palpable.alt_definition: So intense as to be almost able to be felt physically; easily perceptible.
palpable.note: Beyond being touchable, 'palpable' can describe an intense feeling or atmosphere.

preempts.alt_definition: To acquire or appropriate (something) before someone else can.
preempts.note: Besides preventing something, 'preempt' can mean to take something for oneself before another can.

propagate.alt_definition: To spread or promote (an idea, belief, etc.) widely.
propagate.note: Beyond just biological reproduction, it can refer to spreading ideas.

subordinate.alt_definition: To treat or regard as of lesser importance than something else.
subordinate.note: Often used as a noun, but can also be a verb meaning to make something less important.

warranted.alt_definition: To guarantee or attest to the quality or accuracy of.
warranted.note: While it commonly means 'justified,' it can also mean to guarantee something.

compelling.alt_definition: Forcing or driving; exerting a strong, irresistible force.
compelling.note: Beyond just being interesting, it can mean literally forcing someone to do something.

indifference.alt_definition: Mediocrity; a state that is neither good nor bad.
indifference.note: Beyond simple lack of interest, it can imply a neutral or middling quality, which is less obvious.
